sections.title: The single thing they should remember
sections.text: Salvation has two phases, and each camp is perfectly describing one of them. The Calvinist is right about Phase 1 (G moves the coupling function $\alpha(s)$ from zero â€” the dead system cannot generate it). The Arminian is right about Phase 2 (the person moves $s$ from 0 toward +1). One equation, two terms, both real.

sections.title: Critical Insight
sections.text:
### Why it matters now
What MacArthur called an antinomy â€” two truths that appear to contradict but don't â€” is exactly what a clean two-phase process looks like when you only have single-phase language. The equation doesn't replace the preaching. It shows why the preaching was more precise than anyone realized.
__A Note Before We Begin
I have deep respect for John MacArthur and the Reformed tradition. This article doesn't critique his theology — it formalizes it. When MacArthur says _dead in sin_ , that maps to a specific value in a specific equation. When he says _God grants repentance_ , that maps to a specific operation. When he calls sovereignty and responsibility an _antinomy_ — two truths that seem contradictory but aren't — I believe he's exactly right. They aren't contradictory. They're two phases of one process. The equation doesn't replace his preaching. It shows why his preaching is more precise than he may realize.

sections.title: The Enigma
sections.text:
For sixteen hundred years, the church has been having the same argument.
One side says: God chose who would be saved before the world began. Period. The decision was His, made in eternity, based on nothing we did or would do. Some people were written in the book of life before they drew their first breath. Others were not. This isn't cruelty. It's sovereignty. And if it makes you uncomfortable, your discomfort doesn't change what Scripture says.
The other side says: God offers salvation to everyone and the choice is genuinely yours. _Whoever believes_ means whoever. _Choose this day whom you will serve_ means choose. If God decided everything in advance and you had no real say, then every altar call is theater, every prayer of repentance is scripted, and _whosoever will_ is a lie. And God doesn't lie.
1600

sections.title: Phases, One Equation
sections.text: α(s)

sections.title: Coupling Function
sections.text:
Both sides have towering intellects behind them. Augustine, Luther, Calvin, Edwards, Spurgeon, MacArthur — all on the sovereignty side. Arminius, Wesley, Molinists, most of the early church fathers — all on the free will side. Both sides quote Scripture extensively. Both sides accuse the other of ignoring half the Bible.
This isn't a minor theological quibble. This is about the fundamental nature of God's relationship with humanity. Is God a king who decrees, or a father who invites? Is salvation a gift forced into your hands, or an open door you must walk through? Is your prayer of repentance the moment you chose God, or the moment you discovered God had already chosen you?
— The Honest Admission
The Calvinist has Romans 9. The Arminian has 2 Peter 3:9. Both sides quote clear, unambiguous Scripture. Both sides accuse the other of "not really meaning what it says." For sixteen centuries, the church has said: hold them in tension. Accept the mystery.
What if the mystery has an answer?
* * *

sections.title: I. MacArthur's Position, Stated Plainly
sections.text:
John MacArthur sits at the hardest edge of the Reformed tradition. He doesn't soften, hedge, or apologize. His position, in his own words:
The doctrine of election simply means that God, uninfluenced and before creation, predetermined certain people to be saved.
He means it. He builds it on four pillars:
Pillar 1 · God Chooses First
The elect are chosen _before_ the foundation of the world (Ephesians 1:4). Not God peering ahead in time to see who would believe and then "choosing" them retroactively — that would make God's choice contingent on human decision, which makes the human sovereign, not God. God chose. Then the chosen believe. The order matters.
Pillar 2 · Dead Men Don't Choose
Ephesians 2:1: we were "dead in trespasses and sins." Not sick. Not struggling. **Dead.** A dead person doesn't reach for a life preserver. A dead person doesn't respond to an invitation. The first move must come from outside the system entirely. You were a corpse, and God breathed life into you. You didn't ask Him to. You couldn't.
Pillar 3 · The Calling is Effectual
When God calls the elect, they come. Not reluctantly, not under coercion, but freely — because God changes their nature first. MacArthur's analogy: a sheep follows the shepherd because it's a sheep. God made it a sheep. The following is natural, willing, even joyful. But the sheep-making came first, and it wasn't the sheep's idea.
Pillar 4 · And Yet — Responsibility is Real
Here MacArthur surprises people who've only heard caricatures of Calvinism. He simultaneously affirms that "all men are commanded to repent," that "everyone is held culpable and guilty for not repenting," and that "they're unable to respond apart from the intervening sovereign grace of God." All three. At the same time. No apology.
> "God's sovereign election and man's exercise of responsibility in choosing Jesus Christ seem opposite and irreconcilable truths — and from our limited human perspective they are opposite and irreconcilable. That is why so many earnest, well-meaning Christians throughout the history of the church have floundered trying to reconcile them."
— John MacArthur, _Ephesians Commentary_
He holds both. He calls the tension an _antinomy_ — two truths that appear to contradict but don't, because the reconciliation lies beyond our finite comprehension. Let it remain, he says. Believe both. Leave the harmonizing to God.

sections.title: The Pivot
sections.text:
That's an honest answer. It may also be an unnecessary one.
* * *

sections.title: II. The Other Side, Stated Fairly
sections.text: The Arminian position isn't weak, and it isn't based on sentimentality. It's based on Scripture — different Scripture, emphasized differently, but Scripture.

sections.title: __John 3:16
sections.text: "For God so loved **the world**." Not the elect. Not a predetermined subset. The world.

sections.title: __2 Peter 3:9
sections.text: "Not willing that **any** should perish, but that **all** should come to repentance." If God predetermined the outcome, what does _not willing_ mean?

sections.title: __Joshua 24:15
sections.text: "**Choose** this day whom you will serve." Choose. Not "discover who God already chose for you." _Choose._

sections.title: __Revelation 3:20
sections.text:
"Behold, I stand at the door and knock. **If anyone** hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in." Knocking. Waiting. Not breaking the door down.
The Arminian reads these verses and says: the choice is real. Grace makes it possible — no one comes to God without God's initiative — but the response is genuinely the person's. God doesn't override the will. He enables it, invites it, empowers it, but the final _yes_ or _no_ belongs to the human being.
Wesley put it precisely: prevenient grace — grace that comes before, that goes ahead of human choice, that makes the choice possible. Without this grace, no one could respond. But with it, everyone can. The decision is then genuinely the person's. God opens every door. Some walk through. Some don't. And that choice is real, not scripted.
* * *

sections.title: III. Why Neither Side Can Win
sections.text:
Here's the problem, stated with brutal honesty: both sides are reading real Scripture. Both sides are building on verses that clearly say what they say. And both sides, when forced to deal with the other side's best passages, resort to some version of _well, those verses don't mean what they appear to mean._
The Calvinist, confronted with "God is not willing that any should perish," says: _any_ means _any of the elect_. The Arminian, confronted with "He chose us before the foundation of the world," says: _chose_ means _foreknew_. Both are doing the same thing — reading their conclusion into the text to preserve their system.
And both are doing it because they believe the alternative is contradiction. If God really chose the elect before time, then _whoever believes_ can't mean what it seems to mean. If _whoever believes_ really means whoever, then _chose before the foundation of the world_ can't mean what it seems to mean. Both can't be literally true. Can they?
— The Crack in the Stalemate
Both CAN be literally true. Both ARE literally true. They're not describing competing realities. They're describing different phases of the same process. The reconciliation doesn't require divine mystery beyond human comprehension. It requires a specific kind of mathematics that wasn't available until the 20th century.
Both sides are right. About different things. At different moments. In the same equation.
* * *

sections.title: IV. The Resolution, Before the Math
sections.text:
Here's the key insight, stated in plain language before a single variable appears:
Salvation has two phases, and each camp is perfectly describing one of them.
Phase 1 · The Calvinist Is Right

sections.title: When you're spiritually dead
sections.text:
You're not seeking God. You're not reaching out. You're not making a choice because you don't have the capacity to make one. The first move comes from God. It must, because the dead cannot initiate their own resurrection. God reaches in, opens something that was closed, and creates the possibility of response where no possibility existed before. This phase is entirely God.
Phase 2 · The Arminian Is Right

sections.title: After God has opened the door
sections.text:
Now you have a real choice. Grace is flowing. The capacity to respond exists. Within this grace-opened space, you genuinely choose — to surrender deeper, to resist, to move toward God or pull away. The choice is yours. Not because you earned the opportunity (God provided that) but because within the open channel, your response is real.
The two camps aren't contradicting each other. They're describing two sequential phases of the same event and arguing about which phase _is_ salvation. The Calvinist says salvation is Phase 1 — God's sovereign choice to open the door. The Arminian says salvation is Phase 2 — the person's genuine response to walk through it. They're both pointing at real things. They're arguing about the label, not the reality.
Once you see this, the antinomy dissolves. Dead men don't choose (Phase 1 — correct). Whoever believes shall be saved (Phase 2 — correct). God chose before the foundation of the world (Phase 1 — correct). Choose this day whom you will serve (Phase 2 — correct). Every proof text from both sides slots into its proper phase, and none of them need to be reinterpreted.
MacArthur's Counsel, Reread
MacArthur said: leave the harmonizing to God. But what if God has harmonized them — in one equation, with both terms present, both terms necessary, and neither alone sufficient?
* * *

sections.title: V. The Equation
sections.text: For readers who want the formal machinery, here it is. For those who don't, the resolution above stands on its own — the math confirms it but doesn't create it. Skip to _What This Means_ if the symbols aren't your language.

sections.title: Coherence Dynamics
sections.text: $\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$

sections.title: Where:
sections.text:
__
C — Coherence with God
0 to 1. Alignment, closeness, spiritual life.
__
O — Openness
The human reception variable — how much the person is receiving.
__
G — Grace
God's input. Sovereign, external, not generated by the person.
__
S — Sin / Entropy
The decay pressure. Always active post-Fall, always pulling downward.
The growth term is _O · G(1−C)_. The decay term is _S · C_. The factor (1−C) is the room left to grow — the closer you are, the less room remains.
Surrender Coupling $\alpha(s) = \frac{1+s}{2}, \quad s \in [-1, +1]$
**s** = surrender parameter. **−1** = fully closed (autonomous). **0** = threshold. **+1** = fully surrendered.
**Plain English:** The coupling function determines how much of God's grace actually gets through. At s = −1 it equals zero. At s = +1 it equals one.
At **s = −1** (spiritually dead): the coupling function equals zero. No grace gets through. The equation reduces to pure decay: _dC/dt = −S · C_. The system is dying. It cannot reverse its own trajectory. It cannot generate G internally. This is MacArthur's "dead in trespasses and sins." The math proves what he preaches: the first move _must_ come from outside.

sections.title: __Phase 1 · G moves s from −1 toward 0
sections.text: Effectual calling. Irresistible grace. God acts on the closed system to open it. The person at s = −1 didn't choose this — dead things don't choose. G acted on s unilaterally. Monergism: salvation initiated by God alone. Every verb MacArthur uses — _wills, draws, grants, calls, appoints, prepares, causes, chooses_ — describes G acting on a closed system to crack it open.

sections.title: __Phase 2 · The person moves s from 0 toward +1
sections.text:
The channel is open. Grace is flowing. The person has a genuine choice. The O variable is real. It multiplies G. The growth term — _O · G(1−C)_ — requires **both** God's input AND human openness. Neither alone produces growth. This is where _bow down, repent, believe, submit_ lives.
Camp| Describes| s Transition| Mechanism
---|---|---|---
**Calvinist**| Effectual calling| s: −1 → 0| Monergistic — G alone opens the system
**Arminian**| Response / growth| s: 0 → +1| Synergistic — O and G together build coherence
Both right. About different phases. In the same equation.
* * *

sections.title: VI. MacArthur's Specific Claims, Mapped
sections.text: For those who want to see how precisely MacArthur's language maps to the equation:

sections.title: MacArthur Phrase
sections.text:
#### "Dead in trespasses and sins"
= s at −1, coupling function at zero, only the decay term running. **Dynamically dead. Not metaphor.**

sections.title: MacArthur Phrase
sections.text:
#### "God wills, God draws, He grants"
= G initiating the s: −1 → 0 transition. The dead system didn't ask for this. It couldn't. God moved first.

sections.title: MacArthur Phrase
sections.text:
#### "Bow down, repent, believe, submit"
= O increasing as s moves from 0 toward +1. Real human actions. Real agency. Within a space that grace opened.

sections.title: MacArthur Phrase
sections.text:
#### "Irresistible grace"
= G strong enough that the s: −1 → 0 transition always succeeds for the elect. Not coercion — the system at s = −1 has no active resistance. It's just closed. G opens what was shut.

sections.title: MacArthur Phrase
sections.text:
#### "Antinomy"
= what the two-phase process looks like when you only have single-phase language. Once you have the equation, the antinomy dissolves.
* * *

sections.title: VII. The Double Predestination Question
sections.text:
MacArthur rejects double predestination — the idea that God actively sends people to hell. His position: God actively elects the saved and passively "passes by" the rest, allowing them to continue in their own trajectory. The grammatical evidence is striking: Romans 9:22–23 uses a _passive_ verb for "vessels of wrath fitted for destruction" but an _active_ verb for "vessels of mercy, whom He prepared beforehand for glory."
The equation maps this cleanly. At s = −1 with the coupling function at zero, the equation is just _dC/dt = −S · C_. Pure decay. The system decays on its own. No divine push is needed. God doesn't send anyone to hell. He sustains common grace (G > 0 always — the floor we see in the lifespan data) while the system's own dynamics carry it downward.

sections.title: __Active Election
sections.text: God acts on s to produce the −1 → 0 transition. **Active verb.** God prepares, chooses, calls.

sections.title: __Passive Reprobation
sections.text:
The transition doesn't occur. The system stays at s = −1. Decay does its own work. **Passive verb.** Fitted for destruction by its own trajectory.
— The Asymmetry Is Not Arbitrary
Salvation requires external input. Destruction requires only the absence of that input. Romans 9 got the grammar right because the grammar reflects the actual dynamics.
* * *

sections.title: VIII. What Augustine Already Knew
sections.text:
Twelve centuries before this equation was written, Augustine mapped four states of the human will that correspond precisely to specific values:
Augustine's State| Latin Phrase| Equation Value| Description
---|---|---|---
**Before the Fall**| _posse peccare, posse non peccare_| Channel open| Able to sin, able not to sin. Full freedom in both directions.
**After the Fall**| _non posse non peccare_| s = −1, α(s) = 0| Unable not to sin. Locked in decay. MacArthur's "dead in sin."
**Under Grace**| _posse non peccare_| 0 < s < +1| Able not to sin. Channel reopened. Choice restored.
**In Glory**| _non posse peccare_| s = +1, α(s) = 1| Unable to sin. Perfect coupling. Full transparency to grace.
And here's the paradox that troubled everyone: how is _unable to sin_ MORE free than _able to sin or not_?
The equation answers it. At s = +1, the coupling is perfect. No internal resistance. No entropy noise. No competing signals. The person's will and God's will produce identical output — not because the person was overwritten but because they're fully transparent to grace. Paul said it:
> "It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me." — Galatians 2:20
The _freedom_ to choose sin was always the freedom to choose decay. Losing it isn't losing freedom. It's gaining it.

sections.title: The Sheep Analogy, Reread
sections.text:
MacArthur's sheep analogy captures this perfectly. A sheep following the shepherd isn't coerced — it's doing what its nature loves. The "freedom" of running from the shepherd into a wolf's mouth is real freedom: freedom to die. Following the shepherd is also real freedom: freedom to live. **Same will. Different coupling.**
* * *

sections.title: IX. The Hard Question MacArthur Doesn't Answer
sections.text:
To his credit, MacArthur acknowledges what he cannot explain: _Why does God elect some and not others?_
His answer: "The election is based on the secret counsel and good pleasure of His will." Full stop. Romans 9:20 — the potter has authority over the clay.

sections.title: What the Equation Cannot Do
sections.text:
The equation cannot answer this either. It describes the dynamics of _how_ the transition works. It cannot tell you _why_ God initiates it for some and not others. The initial conditions — who receives the effectual call — are set by a Person with reasons the equation doesn't access.
This is honest. The equation dissolves the mechanism of the contradiction. It does not dissolve the mystery of election itself. MacArthur's pastoral humility on this point is mathematically appropriate. Some questions sit outside the equation, inside the character of God.
* * *

sections.title: X. What This Means
sections.text:
Philippians 2:12–13 — the verse MacArthur preaches constantly — states the entire equation in one sentence:
> "Work out your salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you, both to will and to work for His good pleasure."
"Work out your salvation"
= keep the channel open. Maintain your response. This is your job, and it's real.
"With fear and trembling"
= because entropy is always active, always pulling downward, and complacency closes the channel.
"For it is God who works in you"
= G is the power source. You're not generating the coherence. You're receiving it through an open channel.
"Both to will and to work"
= even the willingness to respond is grace-enabled. God provided the opening AND the power AND the desire. Your part is to stay open to what He's already doing.
That's not antinomy. That's one equation. Two terms. Two phases. Both real. Both necessary. Both taught by Scripture for three thousand years, argued about for sixteen centuries, and formalizable in one line of mathematics that was waiting for the vocabulary to catch up with the truth.
— The Final Turn
MacArthur said leave the harmonizing to God. The equation says: He already did.

sections.title: Load-Bearing
sections.text: **Kill if:** a Calvinist or Arminian proof text is shown to require single-phase reading â€” i.e. it cannot be slotted cleanly into Phase 1 (effectual calling, $G$ acting on closed $s$) or Phase 2 (synergistic response, $s$ moving from 0 toward +1) without reinterpretation.

sections.title: Suggestive

sections.title: Destructive

sections.title: Mathematics
sections.text:
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index. Equations remain in the paper body where they were introduced.
Coherence Dynamics $$\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C) - S \cdot C$$
**C** = coherence with God, on [0, 1]. **O** = openness (the human reception variable). **G** = grace (God's input, sovereign and external). **S** = sin / entropy (the decay pressure, always active post-Fall). **(1−C)** = room left to grow.
**Plain English:** Coherence rises with openness times grace acting on what room is left, and falls with sin acting on existing coherence. The article's central equation; both Calvinism and Arminianism are claimed to map onto its two phases.
Surrender Coupling Function $$\alpha(s) = \frac{1 + s}{2}, \quad s \in [-1, +1]$$
**s** = surrender parameter. **−1** = fully closed (autonomous). **0** = threshold. **+1** = fully surrendered. **α(s)** = the fraction of available grace that actually couples to the system.
**Plain English:** Coupling determines how much of God's grace gets through. At s = −1 it equals zero; at s = +1 it equals one. The whole Calvinist–Arminian dispute reduces to which segment of s the speaker is describing.
Decay-Only Regime — Spiritually Dead $$\frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \quad \text{(at } s = -1, \;\alpha(s) = 0\text{)}$$
When the coupling function is zero, the growth term vanishes and only the entropy decay term remains.
**Plain English:** MacArthur's “dead in trespasses and sins” expressed as a closed system. The system cannot reverse its own trajectory or generate G internally. The first move must come from outside.
Phase 1 — Effectual Calling (Monergism) $$G \;\text{acts on}\; s\\!:\; -1 \to 0$$
Unilateral divine action. The closed system has no active resistance — just no openness. Grace opens it.
**Plain English:** God acts on a closed system to crack it open. Calvinism's “irresistible grace”: G is strong enough that the −1 → 0 transition succeeds for the elect. Verbs MacArthur uses — _wills, draws, grants, calls, appoints, prepares_ — all describe G acting on s.
Phase 2 — Synergistic Response $$\text{person acts on}\; s\\!:\; 0 \to +1, \quad \text{growth term} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C)$$
With the channel open, both factors are required: openness and grace. Neither alone produces growth.
**Plain English:** Arminianism's domain. Once grace has opened the channel, the human response is real and necessary — _bow down, repent, believe, submit_. The growth term needs both O and G; setting either to zero stalls the system.
Augustine's Four States — in Equation Form $$\text{Pre-Fall: open} \;\to\; \text{Post-Fall: } s=-1,\,\alpha=0 \;\to\; \text{Under Grace: } 0<s<+1 \;\to\; \text{In Glory: } s=+1,\,\alpha=1$$
Augustine: _posse peccare, posse non peccare_ (pre-Fall) → _non posse non peccare_ (post-Fall) → _posse non peccare_ (under grace) → _non posse peccare_ (in glory).
**Plain English:** Twelve centuries before this equation, Augustine had mapped the four states of the human will. They correspond exactly to four points on the s-axis.
Active Election vs. Passive Reprobation (Romans 9:22–23) $$\text{Election: God acts on}\; s \;\Rightarrow\; \text{active verb} \quad\big|\quad \text{Reprobation: } \frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \;\Rightarrow\; \text{passive verb}$$
Salvation requires external input. Destruction requires only its absence.
**Plain English:** The Greek grammar of Romans 9 reflects the actual asymmetry: vessels of mercy are _prepared_ (active); vessels of wrath are _fitted for destruction_ (passive). The system at s = −1 decays on its own — God doesn't push.
Common Grace as Coherence Floor $$G(t) > 0 \;\text{for all}\; t$$
Even outside the elect, grace is sustained at a positive floor.
**Plain English:** The decay-only regime is not pure starvation. G remains nonzero as common grace — the floor visible across the framework's lifespan and civilizational data — while the unelected system's own dynamics carry it downward.

sections.title: Glossary
sections.text:
Key terms used in this article, defined as the article itself uses them.
Coherence (C)
Alignment, closeness, spiritual life with God on a [0, 1] scale. The state variable in the article's coherence equation; what salvation grows and sin decays.
Openness (O)
The human reception variable. How much of available grace the person is actively receiving. Multiplicative in the growth term — if O = 0, no growth occurs even with full grace.
Grace (G)
God's input. Sovereign, external, not generated by the person. Positive at all times (common grace floor). Strong enough at the elect to drive s from −1 to 0.
Sin / Entropy ($S$)
The decay pressure. Always active post-Fall, always pulling C downward in the equation's decay term S·C. **Framework note:** Sin (theological frame) and Entropy (physical frame) name the same dynamical quantity at the master-equation level. The notation is $S$ either way.
Surrender Parameter (s)
A scalar from −1 (fully closed / autonomous) through 0 (threshold) to +1 (fully surrendered). The hidden axis along which the Calvinist–Arminian debate is staged: each camp describes a different segment.
Coupling Function α(s)
α(s) = (1+s)/2. The fraction of available grace that couples through to the system at a given surrender state. Zero at s = −1; one at s = +1.

sections.title: Phase 1 / Effectual Calling
sections.text: The Calvinist's domain. G acts unilaterally on s to move it from −1 to 0, opening a closed system. Monergistic. The dead system did not ask for or contribute to this.

sections.title: Phase 2 / Response
sections.text: The Arminian's domain. With the channel open, the person moves s from 0 toward +1. Synergistic — both O and G are required for the growth term to fire.

sections.title: Monergism
sections.text: Salvation initiated by God alone. The article reads this as a literally accurate description of Phase 1 — not as a complete description of salvation.

sections.title: Synergism
sections.text: Salvation as cooperative action between God and the person. The article reads this as a literally accurate description of Phase 2 — not as a complete description of salvation.

sections.title: Antinomy
sections.text: MacArthur's term: two truths that appear to contradict but don't. The article's claim: what an antinomy looks like in single-phase language is a clean two-phase process when you have the equation.

sections.title: Election
sections.text: God's choice to initiate the −1 → 0 transition for specific persons. The article holds this is real and asymmetric — God acts (active verb), reprobation occurs by default (passive verb).

sections.title: Predestination
sections.text: God's prior determination of who receives effectual calling. The equation describes the dynamics of _how_ the transition works; it does not access the reasons God elects some and not others.

sections.title: Double Predestination
sections.text: The view that God actively sends some to hell. The article rejects this in line with MacArthur: at s = −1 with α = 0, the equation is pure decay — God does not push the unelected; their own dynamics carry them downward.

sections.title: Active Election / Passive Reprobation
sections.text: The asymmetry the equation exposes: salvation requires external input (active verb in Romans 9), destruction requires only the absence of that input (passive verb).

sections.title: Effectual Calling
sections.text: A divine call that always succeeds in opening the system. In equation terms: G strong enough that the −1 → 0 transition completes. Not coercion — the system at s = −1 has no active resistance, only closure.

sections.title: Irresistible Grace
sections.text: The article's reading: grace at a magnitude that always succeeds in moving s from −1 to 0 for the elect. Not because the will is overridden but because at s = −1 there is nothing actively resisting — only nothing actively receiving.

sections.title: Prevenient Grace
sections.text: Wesley's term: grace that goes ahead of human choice, enabling response without compelling it. The article maps this to the same Phase 1 transition Calvinism calls effectual calling — both names for G acting on a closed s.

sections.title: Common Grace
sections.text: A positive baseline of G sustained for all persons at all times, independent of election. Visible across the framework's lifespan and civilizational data as a coherence floor.

sections.title: Dead in Trespasses and Sins
sections.text: Ephesians 2:1, MacArthur's anchor. The article reads this dynamically, not metaphorically: s = −1, α(s) = 0, only the decay term running. A system that cannot initiate its own resurrection.

sections.title: Calvinist / Reformed
sections.text: The tradition emphasizing divine sovereignty and effectual calling. The article's reading: an accurate description of Phase 1, mistakenly extended over the whole process.

sections.title: Arminian
sections.text:
The tradition emphasizing genuine human response within enabling grace. The article's reading: an accurate description of Phase 2, mistakenly extended over the whole process.
Augustine's Four States
Pre-Fall (_posse peccare, posse non peccare_) → Post-Fall (_non posse non peccare_) → Under Grace (_posse non peccare_) → In Glory (_non posse peccare_). The article maps each to a specific surrender-parameter regime.
In Glory / s = +1
Augustine's _non posse peccare_. Perfect coupling, no internal resistance, no entropy noise. Galatians 2:20: “not I but Christ.” The article's claim: this is gained freedom, not lost — freedom _from_ the freedom-to-decay.

sections.title: Sheep Analogy
sections.text: MacArthur's image: a sheep follows the shepherd because it is a sheep, not because it is coerced. The article reads this as the s = +1 regime — same will, different coupling.

sections.title: Initial Conditions
sections.text: In equation terms, the question of who receives the effectual call. The article admits the equation does not access this; it is set by a Person with reasons outside the dynamics.

sections.title: Antinomy Dissolution
sections.text:
The article's load-bearing claim: with two phases explicit, the proof texts from each camp slot into their proper phase without reinterpretation. The mystery sits at _why God elects_ , not at _how sovereignty and responsibility coexist_.
Philippians 2:12–13
The article's culminating proof text: “work out your salvation with fear and trembling, for it is God who works in you.” Read as the entire equation in one sentence — both terms, both phases, both real.
Logos Field ($\chi$)
The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; the Master-Equation field whose value is total system coherence. (Earlier drafts denoted this with $\Lambda$; that symbol is now reserved for the cosmological constant.) _Field_ ≠ _coupling_ : this entry locks the substrate sense.

sections.title: Logos Coupling
sections.text:
The interaction architecture between the Logos field and the systems embedded in it — the channel structure ($C_0$, $C_1$, inter-substrate coupling) that determines how the field acts on a subsystem. Distinct from the field itself: _field_ = pervasive substrate; _coupling_ = how interaction happens. Both terms stay; they are not interchangeable.
[__Previous: Free Will in Two Frames](../04-free-will-two-frames/gtq-04-free-will-two-frames.html)

equations: \text{person acts on}\; s\\!:\; 0 \to +1, \quad \text{growth term} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C)
\text{Election: God acts on}\; s \;\Rightarrow\; \text{active verb} \quad\big|\quad \text{Reprobation: } \frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \;\Rightarrow\; \text{passive verb}

**C** = coherence with God, on [0, 1]. **O** = openness (the human reception variable). **G** = grace (God's input, sovereign and external). **S** = sin / entropy (the decay pressure, always active post-Fall). **(1−C)** = room left to grow. **Plain English:** Coherence rises with openness times grace acting on what room is left, and falls with sin acting on existing coherence. The article's central equation; both Calvinism and Arminianism are claimed to map onto its two phases. Surrender Coupling Function

**s** = surrender parameter. **−1** = fully closed (autonomous). **0** = threshold. **+1** = fully surrendered. **α(s)** = the fraction of available grace that actually couples to the system. **Plain English:** Coupling determines how much of God's grace gets through. At s = −1 it equals zero; at s = +1 it equals one. The whole Calvinist–Arminian dispute reduces to which segment of s the speaker is describing. Decay-Only Regime — Spiritually Dead

When the coupling function is zero, the growth term vanishes and only the entropy decay term remains. **Plain English:** MacArthur's “dead in trespasses and sins” expressed as a closed system. The system cannot reverse its own trajectory or generate G internally. The first move must come from outside. Phase 1 — Effectual Calling (Monergism)

Unilateral divine action. The closed system has no active resistance — just no openness. Grace opens it. **Plain English:** God acts on a closed system to crack it open. Calvinism's “irresistible grace”: G is strong enough that the −1 → 0 transition succeeds for the elect. Verbs MacArthur uses — _wills, draws, grants, calls, appoints, prepares_ — all describe G acting on s. Phase 2 — Synergistic Response
\text{person acts on}\; s\\!:\; 0 \to +1, \quad \text{growth term} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C)

With the channel open, both factors are required: openness and grace. Neither alone produces growth. **Plain English:** Arminianism's domain. Once grace has opened the channel, the human response is real and necessary — _bow down, repent, believe, submit_. The growth term needs both O and G; setting either to zero stalls the system. Augustine's Four States — in Equation Form

Augustine: _posse peccare, posse non peccare_ (pre-Fall) → _non posse non peccare_ (post-Fall) → _posse non peccare_ (under grace) → _non posse peccare_ (in glory). **Plain English:** Twelve centuries before this equation, Augustine had mapped the four states of the human will. They correspond exactly to four points on the s-axis. Active Election vs. Passive Reprobation (Romans 9:22–23)
\text{Election: God acts on}\; s \;\Rightarrow\; \text{active verb} \quad\big|\quad \text{Reprobation: } \frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \;\Rightarrow\; \text{passive verb}

Salvation requires external input. Destruction requires only its absence. **Plain English:** The Greek grammar of Romans 9 reflects the actual asymmetry: vessels of mercy are _prepared_ (active); vessels of wrath are _fitted for destruction_ (passive). The system at s = −1 decays on its own — God doesn't push. Common Grace as Coherence Floor

Even outside the elect, grace is sustained at a positive floor. **Plain English:** The decay-only regime is not pure starvation. G remains nonzero as common grace — the floor visible across the framework's lifespan and civilizational data — while the unelected system's own dynamics carry it downward. ## Glossary Key terms used in this article, defined as the article itself uses them. Coherence (C) Alignment, closeness, spiritual life with God on a [0, 1] scale. The state variable in the article's coherence equation; what salvation grows and sin decays. Openness (O) The human reception variable. How much of available grace the person is actively receiving. Multiplicative in the growth term — if O = 0, no growth occurs even with full grace. Grace (G) God's input. Sovereign, external, not generated by the person. Positive at all times (common grace floor). Strong enough at the elect to drive s from −1 to 0. Sin / Entropy (

**After the Fall**| _non posse non peccare_| s = −1, α(s) = 0| Unable not to sin. Locked in decay. MacArthur's "dead in sin."

The equation answers it. At s = +1, the coupling is perfect. No internal resistance. No entropy noise. No competing signals. The person's will and God's will produce identical output — not because the person was overwritten but because they're fully transparent to grace. Paul said it:
Coherence Dynamics $$\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C) - S \cdot C$$
Decay-Only Regime — Spiritually Dead $$\frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \quad \text{(at } s = -1, \;\alpha(s) = 0\text{)}$$
Phase 2 — Synergistic Response $$\text{person acts on}\; s\\!:\; 0 \to +1, \quad \text{growth term} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C)$$
